Skip to contents
rkagi 0.1.0
  • Reference
    • rkagi Quickstart Guide

Perform a Kagi summarization in one call

kagi_summarize_once.Rd

This is a high-level convenience wrapper around new_kagi_connection(), new_kagi_summarize(), and kagi_perform(). It constructs a connection, prepares a summarization request, and performs it in a single step.

Usage

kagi_summarize_once(
  url = NULL,
  text = NULL,
  engine = NULL,
  summary_type = NULL,
  target_language = NULL,
  cache = NULL,
  api_key = Sys.getenv("KAGI_API_KEY"),
  base_url = "https://kagi.com/api/v0",
  path = NULL
)

Arguments

url

Optional character scalar. URL to be summarized. Mutually exclusive with text. If both are provided, text takes precedence.

text

Optional character scalar. Raw text to be summarized. Mutually exclusive with url.

engine

Optional character scalar. Summarizer engine to use (e.g. "muriel", "cecil"). If NULL, the API default is used.

summary_type

Optional character scalar. Type of summary requested (e.g. "summary", "takeaway"). If NULL, the API default is used.

target_language

Optional character scalar. Target language for the summary (ISO code such as "EN", "FR", "DE", etc.). If NULL, the API default is used.

cache

Optional logical. Whether to use cached summarizations on the API side. If NULL, the API default is used.

api_key

API key to use for authentication. By default this is read from the KAGI_API_KEY environment variable. For best practice, set Sys.setenv(KAGI_API_KEY="...") or add it to your ~/.Renviron. Advanced users can also pass a function that resolves the key lazily (see new_kagi_connection()).

base_url

Base URL for the Kagi API. Defaults to "https://kagi.com/api/v0". Override only if you are testing against a mock server or a future API version.

path

Optional file path. If supplied, the raw JSON response will be written to this path for reproducibility or debugging.

Value

An object of class kagi_summarize_results. Key components include:

tokens

Token usage information.

meta

List of response metadata (latency, node, API balance).

See also

new_kagi_connection(), new_kagi_summarize(), kagi_perform()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Summarize a URL
res <- kagi_summarize_once(
  url = "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZSRHeXYDLko",
  engine = "muriel",
  summary_type = "summary",
  target_language = "EN"
)

# Summarize raw text
res <- kagi_summarize_once(
  text = "Long input text to be summarized...",
  engine = "cecil",
  summary_type = "takeaway"
)
} # }
